titleStmt.title: No. 1002The United States Representatives on the Economic Subcommittee to the Secretary of State

div.head:
No. 1002
The United States Representatives on the Economic Subcommittee to the Secretary of State
Printed from an unsigned copy prepared by the United States Delegation at Babelsberg.

opener.seg: [ExtractFor the other sections of this report, see document No. 891.]

p.hi: Report to the Secretary on the Work of the Economic Subcommittee

div.p: There is attached the Third Report of the Economic Subcommittee to the Foreign Ministers.

With respect to the question of German external assets, the U. S. and U. K. recommended the inclusion in the Economic PrinciplesAttachment to document No. 863. of the following:
“18. Appropriate steps shall be taken by the Control Council to exercise control and the power of disposition over German owned
external assets not already under the control of United Nations which have taken part in the war against Germany.”
We also recommended that appropriate reference to this matter be made in the communiqué.
The Soviet representatives, while stating that they had very little interest in the matter, reserved their position pending a study of the entire problem.

opener.seg: [Attachment—ExtractFor the other sections of this report, see the attachment to document No. 891, and document No. 1325.]

head.hi: Third Report of the Economic Subcommittee to the Foreign Ministers
head.note: This report was presented at the Eleventh Meeting of the Foreign Ministers, August 1. See ante, p. 562.

frus:attachment.p: The Economic Subcommittee considered the questions of a statement regarding German external assetsSee document No. 1000. and an Article 19See documents Nos. 872, 873, 875, and 876. of the Economic Principles relating to payment for imports into Germany.

With respect to the question of German external assets, no decision was reached. The Soviet representatives, after a general explanation of the problem had been made, requested fuller information and reserved their position pending the furnishing of such information.
